title: mangifera sylvatica
intro: Mangifera sylvatica, also known as the wild mango or Indian horse mango, is a species of mango tree native to the Indian subcontinent. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, diarrhea, and rheumatism, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Mangifera sylvatica include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health issues. Various herbal preparations of Mangifera sylvatica include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
